name: Classic Mexican Flan
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan, melt sugar over moderate heat, stirring frequently until the sugar turns a dark caramel color.
2. Remove from heat and pour into a 4-cup metal ring mold or 8 individual molds. Quickly turn the mold to coat the bottom and sides with the caramel. Allow it to cool so the caramel hardens.
3. Preheat oven to 325°F.
4. In an electric blender, combine condensed milk, milk, egg yolks, eggs, vanilla extract, and almond extract. Blend until well mixed.
5. Pour the mixture into the mold.
6. Place the mold in a larger pan filled with water to a depth of about half an inch.
7. Bake for 1 hour.
8. Remove from the oven and let cool. Refrigerate up to 2 days.
9. Cover the mold with an inverted serving platter. Hold both together and turn them over gently, lifting off the mold. Caramel will fall as a liquid sauce over the custard.
